title: cobblelemon trainer card
description: Cobblelemon Hub mod that unifies my gym, za rank, and guide mod along with a cool trainer card for other players to view.
body:
CobbleLemon Trainer Hub

CobbleLemon Trainer Hub adds a unified in-game hub GUI for CobbleLemon’s Guide, ZA Royale, and Gym League mods, plus a customizable Trainer Card profile system.

Instead of managing several separate GUI keybinds, players can open one hub menu and access supported CobbleLemon features from a single screen. The original direct keybinds can still be customized, but the hub is designed to reduce control clutter and make the connected mods feel more consistent.

Features

- One shared hub screen for supported CobbleLemon mods
- Optional access cards for:
  - CobbleLemon Guide
  - ZA Royale
  - Gym League
  - Trainer Card
- Customizable Trainer Card profile
- Public Trainer Card viewing through the Cobblemon player interact menu
- Cosmetic slots for titles, chat styling, username styling, card frames, backgrounds, badges, and more
- Provider support so other mods can expose cosmetics and Trainer Card stats
- Optional integration with ZA Royale and Gym League stats, ranks, records, roles, and badges
- Designed to expand with future cosmetic types and public profile features

Why Use It?

This mod is useful if your server uses multiple CobbleLemon GUI-based mods and wants a cleaner player experience. It gives players one central place to manage related menus and customize their public Trainer Card without removing direct access options from the individual mods.

The Trainer Card system also gives servers a profile-style cosmetic layer where players can show off ranks, badges, titles, records, and other earned cosmetics.

Important Information

CobbleLemon Trainer Hub is designed as an optional integration mod. The supported CobbleLemon mods can still run without it, and the hub will hide or disable entries for mods that are not installed.

For full functionality, install it alongside the supported CobbleLemon mods and required dependencies, including Fabric, Cobblemon, and any server-side mods used for chat, permissions, or economy integration.
